programmes.show_description: 3/10. Live from the 6 Music Festival.
Live from the 6 Music Festival.
Live from the 6 Music Festival.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Emily Pilbeam presents a mixtape of her personal selection of tracks from BBC Introducing, including a new Track of the Week by Group A.D.
It's Amy Lamé in for Chris Hawkins with a stack of great tunes for your Monday morning.
Deb Grant sits in with wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Lee Brackstone joins Nemone to reflect on Andrew Weatherall's literary legacy.
It's Bank Holiday Monday, and Chris is here to keep the tunes coming, before he shares more Original Pirated Material.
Lammo has a bunch of brand new music to kick start the week and repeats his wonderful 6 Music Festival Indie DJ set, recorded at YES! for 6 Music Festival 2026.
Nathan and Deb unveil this week's Breaking Artist, alt-pop musician Ivy Knight, ahead of the release of her new album Iron Mountain. The Rundown celebrates experimental folk music.
Marc Riley plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Kayus...and his radio alter ego, bring a show full of variety - celebrating Nigerian artists like King Sunny Ade and Sir Shina Peters, 30s Blues from Lil Johnson and Wu-Tang!

programmes.show_name: In Their Own Words: The Prodigy
programmes.show_description: Prodigy band leader Liam Howlett gives Steve Lamacq a studio tour in 2015 around the release of The Day Is My Enemy, and in 2018 they reunite to spin records on the London Eye.
The Prodigy recorded live in concert at the Zap Club, Brighton in 1994.
The Prodigy performing live at the BBC's Maida Vale Studios in London in 2005, and sitting in for Zane Lowe on Radio 1 show in 2009.
Jason Williamson of Sleaford Mods shares his own ultimate Prodigy playlist, with tracks spanning the band's career.
Amy Lamé kick-starts your day with the best new music and forgotten classics. Plus, your chance to vote on a Lost Love.
Actor Hugh Skinner joins Nick to share his favourite music, and chats about the new BBC series Twenty Twenty Six, which sees him reprise his role as Will Humphries from W1A.
Nemone is in for Lauren with the finest blend of fantastic music for your morning.
Dan and Pat from American rock band The Black Keys join Chris to chat about their new album 'Peaches'.
Emily Pilbeam sits in and shines a spotlight on the BBC Introducing Mixtape, and Bonnie Prince Billy has this week's 'Song I Wish I'd Written'.
Contemporary folk six-piece Madra Salach are in session from Maida Vale, performing tracks from their album It's a Hell of an Age, plus a few added extras.
Marc Riley plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Mr Graham 'G' Hasting takes a trip around the globe, discussing the destinations and genres that make up Young Fathers sound, and the joy of sharing music with your pals.
